codeReview.summary: The Ollama streaming completion bug is correctly fixed in backend/services/ollama.ts with the streamDone flag. The fix ensures the loop terminates when either reader.read() returns done:true OR Ollama sends done:true in JSON data. However, the STREAM_TIMEOUT_MS fallback in backend/routes/chat.ts is unreachable dead code - after the for-await loop completes, streamFinished is always true, so the if(!streamFinished) check never executes. This is a non-blocking issue because the core fix in ollama.ts works correctly. The worker also refactored buildChatMessages to buildTokenLimitedContext (moving greeting logic to backend/lib/tokenizer.ts), which is tangentially related to the core fix.

issues.description: The STREAM_TIMEOUT_MS fallback logic at lines 145-151 is unreachable. After the for-await loop completes normally, streamFinished=true is set at line 137, so the if(!streamFinished) check at line 145 is always false. If the loop throws, the catch block returns early at line 141. The timeout fallback will never execute. This is not a blocking issue because the core fix in ollama.ts (streamDone flag) correctly handles streaming completion.

The worker refactored buildChatMessages to buildTokenLimitedContext. This changes how conversation context is built (token-based limiting vs message-count limiting) and moves greeting logic to backend/lib/tokenizer.ts. While this tangentially relates to session quality, it goes beyond the scope of fixing the streaming completion bug. The greeting logic is preserved in tokenizer.ts:115.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend-worker skill requires TDD: 'Write failing unit tests for the service/route before implementation'. The worker did not add new unit tests for the streaming completion fix. The existing streamChat tests only verify it's an async generator and rejects when offline - they don't test the completion scenario with done:true. The handoff shows 312 tests pass but no new tests were added specifically for this fix.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: backend/services/ollama.test.ts:118-127 only tests 'should be an async generator' and 'should reject when Ollama is offline'. No tests for streaming completion behavior.

summary: The Ollama streaming completion bug is correctly fixed in ollama.ts with the streamDone flag ensuring proper loop termination. The STREAM_TIMEOUT_MS fallback in chat.ts is unreachable dead code (non-blocking since core fix works). The worker also performed a refactoring to buildTokenLimitedContext beyond the scope of the core bug fix. No new unit tests were added despite skill requiring TDD.
